title: Chunked Transfer Coding, Extensions, Trailers, and Decoding · Mastery Check
summary: A security review compares near-miss parser decisions for chunked transfer coding, extensions, trailers, and decoding and requires the smallest strict incremental chunked decoder and trailer policy with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for incremental chunked decoder and trailer policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse bounded hexadecimal chunk size with extensions, overflow checks, exact CRLF, and cumulative decoded limits.
Read exactly each chunk-data length plus its terminating CRLF across arbitrary fragmentation.
Treat the last zero-sized chunk as the transition to trailer parsing rather than immediate message completion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Chunked Changed Corpus
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz protocol team handles case 4731 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound chunk accumulation and require trailer validation after the zero chunk; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test extensions, leading zeros, split size lines, missing CRLF, overflow, excess trailer bytes, forbidden fields, and a pipelined next message.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Chunked Changed Corpus.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test extensions, leading zeros, split size lines, missing crlf, overflow, excess trailer bytes, forbidden fields, and a pipelined next message. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Chunked Changed Corpus, keep Chunked Changed Corpus connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
